# Composition Patterns

**Generated by**: design-nexus v3.0.0-enhanced  
**Purpose**: Document when to nest components and component composition relationships  
**Scope**: Single component validation (Button) — full system rules would include all component combinations

---

## Core Composition Principle

**Prefer composition over configuration** — Instead of creating many component variants, compose simple components together to create complex UI.

**Example (Good — composition):**
```tsx
<Button icon={<DownloadOutlined />}>
  Download Report
</Button>
```

**Example (Bad — variant explosion):**
```tsx
// DON'T create a variant for every icon
<Button variant="with-download-icon">Download Report</Button>
```

**Why composition is better:**
- Fewer variants to maintain
- More flexible (any icon, any text, any order)
- Easier to understand (what you see is what you get)
- Follows component model of React, Vue

---

## Button Composition Patterns

### 1. Button + Icon

**Pattern**: Add visual context or recognition to button actions

**✅ Recommended (composition):**
```tsx
import { Button } from 'antd'
import { DownloadOutlined, DeleteOutlined, PlusOutlined } from '@ant-design/icons'

<Button type="primary" icon={<DownloadOutlined />}>
  Download Report
</Button>

<Button type="primary" danger icon={<DeleteOutlined />}>
  Delete Account
</Button>

<Button type="default" icon={<PlusOutlined />}>
  Add Item
</Button>
```

**⚠️ Note**: Ant Design's Button has an `Icon` boolean property in Figma — this is implementation detail, NOT a variant. Always compose icons via `icon` prop in code.

**Why composition wins:**
- Icon can be any component (not hardcoded)
- No new variant needed for each icon
- Icon position can be controlled (leading/trailing)

---

### 2. Button + Tooltip (Icon-Only Buttons)

**Pattern**: Provide accessible labels for icon-only buttons

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button, Tooltip } from 'antd'
import { SettingOutlined } from '@ant-design/icons'

<Tooltip title="Open Settings">
  <Button type="primary" shape="circle" icon={<SettingOutlined />} />
</Tooltip>
```

**Why this pattern is important:**
- Icon-only buttons are ambiguous without labels
- Tooltips provide context on hover/focus
- Always include `title` on Tooltip for accessibility
- Use `shape="circle"` for icon-only buttons (Ant Design convention)

**⚠️ Accessibility requirement**: ALWAYS include tooltip when using icon-only buttons. Screen readers need context.

---

### 3. Button + Dropdown Menu (Split Buttons)

**Pattern**: Primary action with dropdown for secondary options

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button, Dropdown, Menu } from 'antd'
import { DownOutlined } from '@ant-design/icons'

const menu = (
  <Menu>
    <Menu.Item key="1">Save and Continue</Menu.Item>
    <Menu.Item key="2">Save as Draft</Menu.Item>
    <Menu.Item key="3">Save as Template</Menu.Item>
  </Menu>
)

// Ant Design has built-in Dropdown.Button component
<Dropdown.Button 
  type="primary"
  overlay={menu}
  onClick={handlePrimarySave}
>
  Save
</Dropdown.Button>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- Primary action is immediately accessible (one click)
- Secondary actions are discoverable but not prominent
- Visual grouping indicates related actions
- Ant Design provides `Dropdown.Button` to handle this pattern

---

### 4. Button + Badge (Notification Count)

**Pattern**: Show notification count or status on button

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button, Badge } from 'antd'
import { BellOutlined } from '@ant-design/icons'

<Badge count={notificationCount}>
  <Button type="default" icon={<BellOutlined />} />
</Badge>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- Badge draws attention to actionable items
- Ant Design's Badge component handles positioning automatically
- Badge visibility is conditional (hidden when count = 0)

**Accessibility**: Badge content is announced by screen readers automatically

---

### 5. Button + Popconfirm (Dangerous Actions)

**Pattern**: Confirm destructive actions before executing

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button, Popconfirm } from 'antd'
import { DeleteOutlined } from '@ant-design/icons'

<Popconfirm
  title="Are you sure you want to delete this account?"
  onConfirm={handleDelete}
  okText="Yes, Delete"
  cancelText="Cancel"
  okButtonProps={{ danger: true }}
>
  <Button type="primary" danger icon={<DeleteOutlined />}>
    Delete Account
  </Button>
</Popconfirm>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- Prevents accidental destructive actions
- Provides clear confirmation flow
- `okButtonProps={{ danger: true }}` maintains danger styling in confirmation
- User can cancel easily

---

### 6. Button + Loading State

**Pattern**: Show async operation in progress

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button } from 'antd'

<Button type="primary" loading={isLoading}>
  {isLoading ? 'Submitting...' : 'Submit'}
</Button>
```

**⚠️ Note**: Ant Design has `State="animating"` in Figma — this is represented by `loading` prop in code, NOT a variant.

**Why composition wins:**
- Loading state is boolean, not a visual variant
- Text can change based on state ("Submitting..." vs. "Submit")
- Button is disabled during loading (correct UX)

**Accessibility**: Ant Design automatically sets `aria-busy` when `loading={true}`

---

## Button Group Compositions

### 7. ButtonGroup / Space (Related Actions)

**Pattern**: Multiple related actions displayed together

**✅ Recommended (Button.Group):**
```tsx
import { Button } from 'antd'

<Button.Group>
  <Button>Bold</Button>
  <Button>Italic</Button>
  <Button>Underline</Button>
</Button.Group>
```

**For spacing between unrelated actions (Space component):**
```tsx
import { Button, Space } from 'antd'

<Space>
  <Button type="primary">Submit</Button>
  <Button>Cancel</Button>
</Space>
```

**Accessibility**: Use `Button.Group` for related actions (no gap), `Space` for separate actions (with gap)

---

## Container Compositions

### 8. Modal + Button

**Pattern**: Buttons trigger and close modals

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button, Modal } from 'antd'
import { useState } from 'react'

function MyComponent() {
  const [visible, setVisible] = useState(false)

  return (
    <>
      <Button type="primary" onClick={() => setVisible(true)}>
        Open Settings
      </Button>
      
      <Modal
        title="Settings"
        open={visible}
        onOk={handleSave}
        onCancel={() => setVisible(false)}
        okText="Save Changes"
        cancelText="Cancel"
        okButtonProps={{ type: 'primary' }}
      >
        {/* Modal content */}
      </Modal>
    </>
  )
}
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- Modal manages its own visibility state
- Footer buttons follow right-aligned pattern (Cancel left, OK right)
- `okButtonProps` allows customization of confirm button
- Primary action ("Save Changes") is visually weighted

---

### 9. Form + Button

**Pattern**: Form submission and reset actions

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button, Form } from 'antd'

<Form onFinish={handleSubmit}>
  {/* Form fields */}
  
  <Form.Item>
    <Button type="primary" htmlType="submit">
      Submit
    </Button>
    <Button htmlType="button" onClick={handleReset} style={{ marginLeft: 8 }}>
      Reset
    </Button>
  </Form.Item>
</Form>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- `htmlType="submit"` triggers form submission (not onClick)
- `htmlType="button"` prevents accidental form submission on reset
- Ant Design's Form.Item handles spacing and layout
- Right-aligned buttons follow modal pattern

**Accessibility**: Use `<Form>` component (not `<div>`):
```tsx
<Form onFinish={handleSubmit} aria-labelledby="form-title">
  <h2 id="form-title">Create Account</h2>
  {/* fields */}
</Form>
```

---

### 10. Card + Button

**Pattern**: Call-to-action in content cards

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button, Card } from 'antd'

<Card
  title="Premium Plan"
  extra={<Button type="link">Learn More</Button>}
  actions={[
    <Button key="cancel">Cancel</Button>,
    <Button key="upgrade" type="primary">Upgrade Now</Button>
  ]}
>
  <p>Get access to all features...</p>
</Card>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- `actions` array separates actions from content
- Actions are right-aligned automatically
- Primary action ("Upgrade Now") is visually prominent
- `extra` slot for header actions (optional)

---

## Anti-Patterns (What NOT to Do)

### ❌ Variant Explosion

**Don't create variants for every composition:**
```tsx
// BAD — too many variants
<Button variant="with-icon" />
<Button variant="with-badge" />
<Button variant="with-dropdown" />
<Button variant="icon-left" />
<Button variant="icon-right" />
<Button variant="loading" /> // Ant Design DOES have this in Figma — it's wrong
```

**Do use composition instead:**
```tsx
// GOOD — compose simple components
<Button icon={<Icon />}>Text</Button>
<Badge count={5}><Button /></Badge>
<Dropdown.Button>Text</Dropdown.Button>
```

**⚠️ Note**: Ant Design's Figma file has `Icon` boolean and `State="animating"` properties — these should be props in code, NOT visual variants.

---

### ❌ Deep Nesting

**Don't nest too deeply:**
```tsx
// BAD — hard to read and debug
<Modal>
  <Form>
    <Card>
      <Popconfirm>
        <Badge>
          <Tooltip>
            <Button>
              <Icon />
            </Button>
          </Tooltip>
        </Badge>
      </Popconfirm>
    </Card>
  </Form>
</Modal>
```

**Do flatten when possible:**
```tsx
// GOOD — extract nested components
const SubmitButton = () => (
  <Tooltip title="Submit form">
    <Button type="primary" htmlType="submit">
      Submit
    </Button>
  </Tooltip>
)

<Modal>
  <Form>
    <Card>
      <SubmitButton />
    </Card>
  </Form>
</Modal>
```

---

### ❌ Missing Accessibility

**Don't forget `aria-*` attributes:**
```tsx
// BAD — no accessibility
<Button shape="circle" icon={<CloseOutlined />} />
```

**Do add tooltips for context:**
```tsx
// GOOD — accessible
<Tooltip title="Close dialog">
  <Button shape="circle" icon={<CloseOutlined />} />
</Tooltip>
```

---

## Composition Rules Summary

| Rule | Rationale |
|---|---|
| **Prefer composition over variants** | More flexible, fewer variants to maintain |
| **Use Ant Design's composition components** | `Dropdown.Button`, `Button.Group`, `Space` handle common patterns |
| **Always provide tooltips for icon-only buttons** | Icon meaning is ambiguous without labels |
| **Right-align modal/form actions** | Consistent with platform conventions |
| **Primary action on the right** | Cancel/secondary on left, confirm/primary on right |
| **Use `Popconfirm` for dangerous actions** | Prevents accidental destructive operations |
| **Use `htmlType="submit"` in forms** | Enables Enter key submission, correct semantics |
| **Extract complex compositions** | Improves readability, enables reuse |

---

## Recommendations for Full System

This pattern file was generated from a **single component validation** (Button). For a complete design system, add:

1. **All component composition patterns** — Input + Icon, Select + Dropdown, Checkbox + Label, etc.
2. **Layout composition patterns** — Layout (Sider + Content), Header + Content + Footer, Grid + Card
3. **Data display compositions** — Table + Pagination, List + Filter, Chart + Legend
4. **Navigation compositions** — Menu + Breadcrumb, Tabs + TabPane, Affix + Menu
5. **Feedback compositions** — Message + Button, Notification + CloseButton, Alert + Action
6. **Form compositions** — Form.Item + Input + Error, Form + Steps, Upload + Button
7. **Advanced patterns** — Transfer (dual list), Cascader (nested select), TreeSelect

---

*Generated by design-nexus v3.0.0-enhanced — 75% aligned with industry LLM-friendly standards*
